content_hints.description: The refugee camp sprawls outside Plainsworth's walls like a wound that will not close. Families huddle under lean-tos made from cart covers and broken furniture. Cook-fires burn low, fueled by whatever scraps can be found. Children stand in line at a communal water barrel, too quiet for their age. The walls of Plainsworth loom above, gates half-shut.
content_hints.read_aloud: The camp smell hits you first -- smoke, unwashed bodies, and the sour edge of desperation. Hundreds of faces turn toward you as you approach. They have learned to watch for uniforms.

npcs.name: Vayla Shen
npcs.dialogue: You're Legion? Then you should know -- we came from the west road three days ago. There were supply wagons behind us, but we heard fighting and they never arrived. Someone should look into that. Someone with swords.
npcs.notes: Vayla led forty refugees from her village when the Rotters came. She lost her daughter on the road and will not speak of it. She has a sharp memory for terrain and troop movements -- a former surveyor.

npcs.name: Sunpriest Kevarin
npcs.dialogue: The Panyar shrine south of here has gone silent. I sent two acolytes to check on it and neither returned. The land there feels wrong -- heavy, like the air before a thunderstorm that never breaks. Something of Blighter's corruption has taken root.
npcs.notes: Kevarin carries a relic fragment wrapped in oilcloth inside his vestments -- a shard of the Shattered Sun. He believes it can cleanse corrupted holy sites but has never tested the theory.

event_triggers: A child tugs at your sleeve. 'Are you going to make the bad things go away?' She does not wait for an answer.

content_hints.description: A canvas pavilion serves as Kevarin's field temple. Prayer beads hang from the tent poles. A circle of river stones marks the sanctified ground where the wounded are laid for blessing. The air smells of sage and the sharp mineral tang of consecrated water.
content_hints.read_aloud: A canvas pavilion, cleaner than anything else in the camp. Prayer beads click softly from the tent poles. Inside, a circle of river stones marks consecrated ground, and the air smells of sage and something sharp and mineral -- blessed water, poured recently.

npcs.name: Sunpriest Kevarin
npcs.dialogue: Rest here if you need it. The blessings are thin, but they hold. If you mean to cleanse the shrine, come speak to me first -- I have something that may help.

content_hints.description: The camp's supply point is a trestle table under a patched awning. Refugees barter what they carried from their villages -- tools, cloth, preserved food. A Bartan trader named Ossik has set up a scale and does brisk business converting valuables into rations at rates that would be criminal anywhere else.
content_hints.read_aloud: A trestle table under a patched awning serves as the camp's market. Refugees line up to trade heirlooms and tools for rations. A trader with a brass scale weighs everything twice, his smile never reaching his eyes. The exchange rates are written on a slate board. They are not kind.
content_hints.investigation_success: Ossik is selling information to both the Legion and an unknown third party. His ledger has entries in cipher that match no merchant's shorthand.
